highlights.text: It is also why I wrote last Wednesday at 4pm: “Bear Case Thursday: Begins on the failure of 6758”.

I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “My general lean is ES can try and recover 6639 to generate a rally to 6693, 6704, 6716, 6739, then 6758-65.” We got started right on this after 6pm last evening and by 7pm we recovered 6639 and ran to 6716+ target exactly per plan.
Thursday morning, price picked its path, and down we went ultimately into 6640 early Friday morning going elevator down .
Every elevator down sell sees an equally violent squeeze the other way.
Those squeezes however, only occur when price triggers my core setup: The Failed Breakdown which occurs when price flushes a major low, traps shorts, then reverses.
I wrote Thursday at 4pm: “The much safer entry is the Failed Breakdown of Monday’s 10:10AM 6641 low.

This is a huge low from which we ran into this weeks highs and the Failed Breakdown is obviously actionable.” Around 4:15AM Friday ES lost 6641 by a point, recovered, and ripped around 100 points into Friday’s 6738 high of day, from which we sold into Friday’s 6620s low.
1) To start a squeeze, they’d need to put in a Failed Breakdown and to do this, theyd need to recover 6639 which was a big shelf all day Friday.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow

post_title: Another Bounce For SPX. Will It Get Sold Like The Others? March 17 Plan
